Entries.UsedFor: Canonical product name 'QuickBooks Online' and product home page (NavigationBaseURL).
Entries.Excerpt: QuickBooks Online product home page; Intuit's canonical name for the SaaS accounting product.

Entries.UsedFor: Confirms QBO is a REST/JSON API and the v3 path versioning strategy.
Entries.Excerpt: Get Started portal: base URLs https://quickbooks.api.intuit.com/v3/company/{realmId} (production) and https://sandbox-quickbooks.api.intuit.com (sandbox); v3 path versioning.

Entries.UsedFor: OAuth 2.0 authorization-code flow; scopes (com.quickbooks.accounting, com.quickbooks.payments); access token TTL 1hr; refresh token TTL 5yr / 100-day idle; refresh-token rotation.
Entries.TargetField: integration.CredentialTypeID | configuration.Auth.Strategy | configuration.Auth.Scopes | configuration.Auth.AccessTokenTTLSeconds | configuration.Auth.RefreshTokenTTLSeconds | configuration.Auth.RefreshTokenIdleExpiryDays | configuration.Auth.RefreshTokenRotation | configuration.Auth.TokenRefreshStrategy | configuration.Auth.AuthHeaderPattern
Entries.Excerpt: QuickBooks Online uses OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code grant. Scopes: com.quickbooks.accounting (Accounting, Reports, CDC, Batch, Webhooks) and com.quickbooks.payments (Payments). Access tokens valid for 1 hour; refresh tokens valid for ~5 years with 100-day idle expiry (rotated on each refresh).

Entries.UsedFor: Token lifecycle edge cases (refresh-token rotation semantics, idle expiry).
Entries.Excerpt: OAuth FAQ: refresh tokens are rotated on each use and expire after 100 days of inactivity (with a 5-year absolute lifetime).

Entries.UsedFor: Production OAuth/OIDC discovery document (token + authorization + revocation endpoints).
Entries.Excerpt: Intuit OpenID discovery document publishes canonical endpoints: authorization_endpoint, token_endpoint, revocation_endpoint.

Entries.UsedFor: Rate-limit facts (500 req/min/realm standard, 40 req/min batch, 200 req/min heavy, 10 concurrent, HTTP 429 + Retry-After).
Entries.Excerpt: API call limits: 500 requests/minute/realm for standard endpoints; 40 requests/minute for the Batch endpoint; 200 requests/minute for heavy endpoints; max 10 concurrent. 429 with Retry-After when exceeded.

Entries.UsedFor: Pagination via STARTPOSITION (1-based) + MAXRESULTS (max 1000); query language; minor-version query param; JSON/XML content-type duality.
Entries.Excerpt: REST API features: pagination uses STARTPOSITION (1-based) and MAXRESULTS clauses in the query string; max page size is 1000. Minor-version pinning via ?minorversion=N. Content negotiation supports application/json and application/xml.

Entries.UsedFor: Batch endpoint shape; 30-item max; BatchItemRequest/BatchItemResponse envelope; serial execution; partial-success support.
Entries.Excerpt: Batch API: BatchItemRequest array (max 30 items/request), each with bId, operation (create/update/delete/query). Server returns BatchItemResponse[] with per-item results or Fault errors.

Entries.UsedFor: CDC endpoint, query params (entities + changedSince), 30-day window cap, ISO8601 watermark format.
Entries.Excerpt: /v3/company/{realmId}/cdc accepts entities=<comma list> and changedSince=<ISO8601>. Returns all changes in a single response. 30-day window cap; older requests rejected.

Entries.UsedFor: Webhook configuration (developer-portal level), intuit-signature HMAC-SHA256 base64 header, payload shape (eventNotifications[].dataChangeEvent.entities[]), supported operations.
Entries.Excerpt: Webhooks: subscribed via the developer-portal app config (not a tenant API). Payload signed via intuit-signature header (HMAC-SHA256, base64 encoded). Payload shape: eventNotifications[].dataChangeEvent.entities[] with name/id/operation/lastUpdated. Operations: Create, Update, Delete, Merge, Void, Emailed.

Entries.UsedFor: Webhook signature verification details (companion to webhooks doc).
Entries.Excerpt: Webhooks help article: signature is HMAC-SHA256 of the raw request body using the app's verifier token, base64-encoded, in the intuit-signature header.

Entries.UsedFor: Fault envelope shape (Fault.Error[]); 5010 Stale Object error (SyncToken concurrency).
Entries.Excerpt: Errors returned in Fault envelope: {Fault: {Error: [{Message, Detail, code, element}], type}}. Error 5010 = Stale Object (SyncToken mismatch) on updates.

Entries.UsedFor: Custom-field discovery via Preferences entity (SalesFormsPrefs.CustomField, VendorAndPurchasesPrefs.CustomField).
Entries.Excerpt: Preferences endpoint exposes per-realm SalesFormsPrefs.CustomField[] (and similar) — the catalog of admin-defined custom fields on Sales transactions (max 3 per form).

Entries.UsedFor: CompanyInfo entity as a universal health-check / TestConnection target.
Entries.Excerpt: CompanyInfo entity is universally available on every QBO realm. GET /v3/company/{realmId}/companyinfo/{realmId} returns the realm profile.

Entries.UsedFor: Vendor-specific FK convention: CustomerRef.{value,name} nested-object pattern; representative of QBO-wide FK shape.
Entries.Excerpt: Invoice.CustomerRef is a nested reference object: { value: <Customer Id string>, name: <display name> }. This *Ref pattern is uniform across QBO entities (VendorRef, AccountRef, ItemRef, etc.).

Entries.UsedFor: Establishes Payments API as a separate product on api.intuit.com host with com.quickbooks.payments scope — explicitly out of scope for THIS connector.
Entries.Excerpt: QuickBooks Payments developer portal: separate API surface, separate host (api.intuit.com), distinct OAuth scope (com.quickbooks.payments).

Entries.UsedFor: Official Intuit-maintained .NET SDK — XSD-generated entity classes are the authoritative machine-readable schema source. Used as ground-truth for SyncToken pattern + nested-Ref FK shape since no OpenAPI exists.
Entries.Excerpt: intuit/QuickBooks-V3-DotNET-SDK: XSD-derived entity classes for the full Accounting catalog. SyncToken field present on every mutable entity; *Ref nested-object pattern uniform across FKs.

Entries.UsedFor: In-tree connector confirms ImportPath=@memberjunction/connectors, ClassName=QuickBooksConnector, production+sandbox base hosts, OAuth refresh flow, SELECT-with-STARTPOSITION/MAXRESULTS query pattern.
Entries.Excerpt: QuickBooksConnector.ts constants: QBO_PRODUCTION_BASE = 'https://quickbooks.api.intuit.com', QBO_SANDBOX_BASE = 'https://sandbox-quickbooks.api.intuit.com'. Refresh flow posts grant_type=refresh_token to oauth.platform.intuit.com. Query construction uses STARTPOSITION + MAXRESULTS clauses.

Entries.UsedFor: Authoritative source establishing that QuickBooks Online uses OAuth 2.0 (Authorization Code grant with refresh-token rotation) as its sole authentication mechanism — directly justifies the integration.CredentialTypeID lookup to 'OAuth2'.
Entries.Excerpt: All apps integrating with QuickBooks Online use OAuth 2.0 (authorization code grant) for authentication and authorization. No alternative credential type is supported.

Entries.UsedFor: Authoritative source establishing the per-request batch-item cap of 30 — directly justifies integration.BatchMaxRequestCount=30.
Entries.Excerpt: Batch endpoint accepts a BatchItemRequest array with a maximum of 30 items per request. Requests exceeding 30 items are rejected with an error.

Entries.UsedFor: Canonical product name 'QuickBooks Online' as published by Intuit on the product home page — establishes integration.Name.
Entries.Excerpt: QuickBooks Online — Intuit's canonical SaaS accounting product name as displayed on the official product home page.

Entries.UsedFor: Product home page URL — establishes integration.NavigationBaseURL.
Entries.Excerpt: https://quickbooks.intuit.com/online/ is the canonical entry point for QuickBooks Online users; appropriate destination for the integration's navigation link.

Entries.UsedFor: In-tree connector class file confirms the registered class name — establishes integration.ClassName.
Entries.Excerpt: QuickBooksConnector.ts declares `export class QuickBooksConnector extends BaseRESTIntegrationConnector` with @RegisterClass(BaseIntegrationConnector, 'QuickBooksConnector').

Entries.UsedFor: Connector package's npm name — establishes integration.ImportPath as '@memberjunction/connectors'.
Entries.Excerpt: packages/Integration/connectors/package.json declares `"name": "@memberjunction/connectors"` — the canonical TypeScript import path for the QuickBooksConnector class.

Entries.UsedFor: Font Awesome icon catalog — confirms 'fa-file-invoice-dollar' is a valid Font Awesome icon class and is the most semantically appropriate icon for an accounting / invoicing integration (QuickBooks).
Entries.Excerpt: fa-file-invoice-dollar — official Font Awesome icon depicting an invoice with a dollar sign. Selected for QuickBooks Online to visually represent its primary domain (accounting / invoicing).

Entries.UsedFor: Authoritative product/platform description compiled from Intuit's developer portal Get Started page — sources the integration.Description prose (REST/JSON API v3, OAuth 2.0, base URL pattern, scope model, pagination, batch, webhooks, CDC).
Entries.Excerpt: QuickBooks Online API v3 is a REST/JSON API. Production base URL: https://quickbooks.api.intuit.com/v3/company/{realmId}; sandbox: https://sandbox-quickbooks.api.intuit.com. OAuth 2.0 authorization-code flow; com.quickbooks.accounting (Accounting/Reports/CDC/Batch/Webhooks) and com.quickbooks.payments (separate Payments product on qbpayments host) scopes.
